'''Jermaine McGillvary''' (born 16 May 1988) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}}er. He spent the majority of his club career with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the [[Super League]],  but also spent time on loan at [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] and [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He finished his career with [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. 

At international level, he represented the [[England Knights]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].

==Background==
McGillvary was born in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, and he is of [[Grenada|Grenadian]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/news/west-yorkshire-news/i-save-cheers-jermaine-meet-12189018 |title="I save all my cheers for Jermaine": Meet Jermaine McGillvary's biggest fans |publisher=Huddersfield Examiner |date=20 November 2016 |access-date=20 November 2016}}</ref> He played junior [[rugby league]] for Deighton, but quit the sport after the club folded, and switched to [[association football|football]] instead, playing for Dalton Dynamoes,<ref>{{cite news |title=Dynamoes joy with late goal |url=https://www.thefreelibrary.com/Dynamoes+joy+with+late+goal.-a0116183007 |access-date=11 September 2023 |work=Huddersfield Daily Examiner |date=6 April 2004 |via=''[[The Free Library]]''}}</ref> and at semi-professional level for [[Emley A.F.C.|Emley]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Guest Column - Jermaine McGillvary: I wouldn't be ready for the top flight without my spells on loan |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/guest-column-jermaine-mcgillvary-i-wouldnt-be-ready-for-the-top-flight-without-my-spells-on-loan-1972214 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=11 September 2023 |date=11 June 2010}}</ref> He was working as an apprentice bricklayer when he was persuaded by his cousin, [[Leroy Cudjoe]], to return to rugby league, and he began training with the [[Huddersfield Giants]] reserve team.<ref>{{cite web |title=New boy McGillvary amazed at progress |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/new-boy-mcgillvary-amazed-progress-5033083 |website=YorkshireLive |access-date=11 September 2023 |date=15 August 2008}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
After impressing in the reserves and a successful loan spell at [[Batley Bulldogs]], McGillvary signed a full-time contract with the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in August 2008.<ref>{{cite web |title=Super Giants deal for winger McGillvary |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/super-giants-deal-winger-mcgillvary-5033107 |website=YorkshireLive |access-date=11 September 2023 |date=15 August 2008}}</ref> McGillvary was sent back out on loan to Batley for the [[2009 RFL Championship|2009 Championship]] season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Jermaine is heading back to Bulldogs |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/jermaine-heading-back-bulldogs-5032360 |website=YorkshireLive |access-date=11 September 2023 |date=29 September 2008}}</ref> He scored 21 tries for the club during the season, including five in a single match against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] on 24 May 2009, equalling the club record.<ref>{{cite web |title=Club Records |url=https://www.batleybulldogs.co.uk/club-records/ |website=Batley Bulldogs |access-date=11 September 2023}}</ref> His performances earned him a nomination for the Young Player of the Year award, and he was named in the 2009 Championship team of the season.<ref>{{cite book |editor1-last=Butcher |editor1-first=Tim |editor2-last=Spencer |editor2-first=Daniel |title=Gillette Rugby League Yearbook 2009-2010 |date=2009 |publisher=League Publications |isbn=978-1-901347-21-0 |page=96 |url=https://archive.org/details/gilletterugbylea0000unse/page/318/mode/2up?view=theater}}</ref>

McGillvary spent the first part of the 2010 season with [[Barrow Raiders]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Homegrown Huddersfield Giants winger McGillvary relishing Bradford Bulls test |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/homegrown-huddersfield-giants-winger-mcgillvary-4971828 |website=YorkshireLive |access-date=11 September 2023 |date=14 July 2011}}</ref> scoring eight tries in 11 appearances.<ref>{{cite web |title=England Four Nations call for ex-Barrow winger McGillvary |url=https://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16451641.england-four-nations-call-for-ex-barrow-winger-mcgillvary/ |website=The Mail |access-date=11 September 2023 |date=10 October 2016}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] début for Huddersfield in June 2010, scoring two tries in a 52–6 win against [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Huddersfield Giants' Jermaine on dream Super League debut v Bradford Bulls |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-jermaine-dream-super-4995963 |website=YorkshireLive |access-date=11 September 2023 |date=7 June 2010}}</ref>

McGillvary's breakthrough came in 2011, starting all but one game for Huddersfield during the season, and scoring 17 tries.<ref>{{cite news |title=McGillvary proud of his award |url=https://www.thefreelibrary.com/McGillvary+proud+of+his+award.-a0266927391 |access-date=11 September 2023 |work=Huddersfield Daily Examiner |date=14 September 2011 |via=''[[The Free Library]]''}}</ref> He signed a new five-year contract with the club,<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14543575|title = McGillvary signs new Giants deal|work = BBC Sport|date=16 August 2011}}</ref> and was awarded the Albert Goldthorpe Rookie of the Year Medal.<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|access-date=16 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140326074057/http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|archive-date=26 March 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In the [[2013 Super League season]], McGillvary played 27 games and scored 19 tries as Huddersfield claimed the [[League Leaders Shield]] for the first time in 81 years.  Huddersfield would ultimately fall short of a grand final appearance that year.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23850876|title=Huddersfield Giants 40-0 Wakefield|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 4 May 2014, McGillvary made his 100th appearance for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. Fittingly he scored a try in his team's crucial one point win over third placed [[Super League]] team, the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report/9980|title=RUGBY-LEAGUE.COM|website=Rugby-League.com|access-date=9 October 2017|archive-date=24 September 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924131725/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report/9980|url-status=dead}}</ref>

McGillvary finished as Super League's top try scorer in 2015, with 27, and earned selection in the [[Super League Dream Team]].{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}
On 4 October 2020, McGillvary scored two tries in a 32–22 victory over [[Hull KR]]. In the process, he moved into the Super League's top ten highest ever try scorers list.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/mcgillvary-joins-super-leagues-top-ten/|title=McGillvary joins Super League's top ten|website=www.totalrl.com|date=4 October 2020}}</ref>

In round 15 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored four tries in Huddersfield's 40–26 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/huddersfield-vs-hull/55652/|title= Four tries from Jermaine McGillvary help Huddersfield Giants to 40-26 win over Hull FC|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
In the 2022 Challenge Cup semi-final, McGillvary earned man of the match honours as Huddersfield defeated [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] 25–4 at [[Elland Road]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jermaine-mcgillvary-always-stayed-loyal-huddersfield-giants/|title=Jermaine McGillvary on why he has always stayed loyal to Huddersfield|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=8 May 2022 }}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, McGillvary played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].  McGillvary scored a second half try during the match.  In round 18 of the [[Super League XXVII]] season, McGillvary scored two tries for Huddersfield in a 30–18 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at [[Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>
McGillvary played 13 games in the 2023 Super League season and scored six tries as Huddersfield finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity ===
On 28 October 2023 he joined [[Wakefield Trinity]] for the 2024 season. He went on to win the [[RFL 1895 Cup]] and the [[RFL Championship Leaders' Shield]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League |date=28 October 2023|title=Wakefield Trinity pull off Jermaine McGillvary coup: ‘Hopefully I can help the club reach Super League’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-trinity-pull-off-jermaine-mcgillvary-coup-hopefully-i-can-help-the-club-reach-super-league}}</ref>
McGillvary's final game as a player was the RFL Championship grand final against [[Toulouse Olympique]] where Wakefield would win 36-0. McGillvary would kick a goal from the sideline towards the closing stages of the match.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqjr5lvxk4lo|title=Jowitt breaks points record as Wakefield cruise to Grand Final win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
McGillvary's was selected in Steve McNamara's 24-man [[England national rugby league team|England team]] for their [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|test series against New Zealand]] in 2015.<ref>{{cite news 
|title = MCNAMARA NAMES 24-MAN SQUAD FOR 2015 INTERNATIONAL SERIES
|publisher = englandrl.co.uk
|date = 2015-10-11
|url = http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article/47305/mcnamara-names-24-man-squad-for
|access-date = 2015-10-24}}</ref> He made his début for England in the decisive final test-match at the DW Stadium.

The following year, McGillvary was selected in the England squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]]. Before the tournament, England played a [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|test match]] against France in which McGillvary scored a try in England's 40–6 win.

Following the 2017 season, McGillvary was named in Wayne Bennett's England squad for the [[Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] in Australia.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-jermaine-mcgillvary-makes-13735993|title=Huddersfield Giants Jermaine McGillvary makes England squad|last=Barrow|first=Pete|date=2017-10-09|work=huddersfieldexaminer|access-date=2017-10-13}}</ref><ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

McGillvary played on the wing in England's 6–0 defeat by Australia in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]] final.  He finished the tournament with seven tries in five games and ran more meters than any other player in the tournament.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/world-cup/australia-capped-off-a-stunning-rlwc-with-the-team-of-the-tournament/news-story/fc74c6780911754d841373439494ce9c|title = Australia, England dominate RLWC Team of the Tournament|newspaper = News.com.au|date = 3 December 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants/world-cup-final-england-s-jermaine-mcgillvary-hopes-to-hit-right-note-in-grand-finale-1-8889161| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20171204171203/https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants/world-cup-final-england-s-jermaine-mcgillvary-hopes-to-hit-right-note-in-grand-finale-1-8889161| archive-date = 2017-12-04| title = World Cup Final: England's Jermaine McGillvary hopes to hit right note in grand finale - Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/rugby-league-world-cup-2017-australia-scrape-out-60-victory-in-final-against-wayne-bennettinspired-england-20171202-gzxh7d.html|title=Rugby League World Cup 2017: Australia scrape out 6-0 victory in final against Wayne Bennett-inspired England|date=2 December 2017}}</ref>

In 2018 he was selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].<ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|newspaper=The Telegraph|publisher=Guardian|date=17 October 2018|access-date=31 October 2019|last1=Jolly|first1=Richard}}</ref>

He was selected in [[England 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref name="Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-tomkins-eager-lift-inaugural-20595184|title=Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under|publisher=Mirror|date=16 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|newspaper=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019|last1=Heppenstall|first1=Ross}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite news|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|publisher=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019|last1=Irvine|first1=Chris}}</ref>

On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]], and scored a try, in their 26–24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation. McGillvary subsequently announced his international retirement in June 2022.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://uk.news.yahoo.com/jermaine-mcgillvary-announces-international-retirement-132500680.html | title=Jermaine McGillvary announces international retirement }}</ref>
